repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 2 Series 230i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replacement often £3,800–£6,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 2 Series 230i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles)
* Cooling system history — water pump and expansion tank checked
* No whistling from the engine (PCV valve) and no coolant loss
* G42 (post-2021) if you want the latest refinement and power
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating (cooling system risk)
* Whistling noise from the engine (PCV valve failure)
* Poor service history — the B48 is robust but needs proper oil
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 230i is a genuine driver's car — a light, rear-driven coupe with BMW's excellent B48 four-cylinder. It's a significant step forward from the N20, with timing chain issues virtually eliminated. Cooling system maintenance is the key to longevity — a £400–£1,000 water pump replacement is small change on a car worth £15,000–£35,000. Buy on history, maintain the cooling system, and the 230i will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 2 Series 230i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 2 Series 230i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle; the B48 should start smoothly
* Service History — 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes are essential for the B48
* Coolant Level — Check for any loss — water pump and expansion tank are key risks
* PCV Valve — Any whistling from the engine? Increased oil consumption?
* Oil Filter Housing — Any oil leaks at the front of the engine?
* VANOS Operation — Check low-end torque and smooth power delivery
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures

items.question: How much does a BMW 2 Series 230i engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW 2 Series 230i engine (B48B20) typically costs £3,800–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500. These are specialist-only replacements — the B48 requires BMW specialist expertise. A water pump replacement costs £400–£1,000.

items.question: Is the BMW 2 Series 230i reliable?
items.answer: Yes — the B48 is a robust and well-developed engine that's significantly improved over the N20. The timing chain is now front-mounted, virtually eliminating the N20's chain issues. Cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank) and PCV valve/oil separator issues are the main concerns. Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) are essential.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW 2 Series 230i?
items.answer: Cooling system leaks — water pump and expansion tank failure (£400–£1,000), PCV valve/oil separator failure (whistling noise, oil consumption, £400–£800), and oil filter housing gasket leaks (£500–£1,200). The B48 is otherwise exceptionally robust.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW 2 Series 230i engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: 2 Series 230i values range from £12,000–£35,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £3,800–£6,500 reconditioned engine represents 15–55% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained G42 example, replacement is often justified. For an ageing F22 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

items.question: How do I find my BMW 2 Series 230i engine code?
items.answer: The 230i uses the B48B20 engine code — BMW's 2.0-litre four-cylinder turbo. F22/F23 (2014–2020) is the earlier 245bhp variant; G42 (2021–present) is the 255bhp version. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the cooling system leak, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — water pump and expansion tank replacement (£400–£1,000) is a straightforward repair on the B48. The water pump is known to fail with age. Replacing both the water pump and expansion tank together is recommended — they degrade at similar rates. At the first sign of coolant loss or sweet smell, book an inspection immediately — ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating and serious engine damage.
